_comment: Seed data for Uni Match SG. All IGP, salary, and employment figures are approximate and should be verified against official sources (NUS/NTU/SMU IGP pages, MOE GES surveys) before production use. IGP rank points are on the 70-point scale. Salary and employment data referenced from MOE Graduate Employment Survey (GES) 2023.

courses.name: Computer Science
courses.description: NUS Computer Science provides a strong foundation in software engineering, algorithms, artificial intelligence, computer systems, and data science. The programme is consistently ranked among the top in Asia and produces graduates highly sought after by the tech industry.

courses.name: Business Administration
courses.description: NUS Business Administration offers a broad-based business education covering finance, marketing, management, strategy, and analytics. Students can specialise in areas such as finance, marketing, or management and organisations.

courses.name: Medicine
courses.faculty: Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine
courses.description: NUS Medicine is a five-year undergraduate programme that trains students to become competent and compassionate doctors. The curriculum integrates basic medical sciences with clinical training at the National University Hospital and other affiliated hospitals.

prerequisites.requirementText: H2 Chemistry and either H2 Biology or H2 Physics

courses.name: Law
courses.description: NUS Law offers a four-year LLB programme that provides rigorous training in legal analysis, reasoning, and practice. The faculty is one of the most prestigious law schools in Asia with strong mooting and clinical legal education programmes.

prerequisites.requirementText: Strong GP / Knowledge and Inquiry grades preferred

courses.name: Civil Engineering
courses.description: NUS Civil Engineering prepares students for careers in structural, geotechnical, transportation, and environmental engineering. The programme emphasises sustainable infrastructure development and smart city technologies.

courses.name: Electrical Engineering
courses.description: NUS Electrical Engineering covers power systems, electronics, telecommunications, and control systems. Students gain skills in circuit design, signal processing, and embedded systems relevant to modern technology industries.

courses.name: Mechanical Engineering
courses.description: NUS Mechanical Engineering covers thermodynamics, mechanics, materials science, and manufacturing. Students learn to design and analyse mechanical systems for industries including aerospace, automotive, and energy.

courses.name: Chemical Engineering
courses.description: NUS Chemical Engineering combines chemistry, physics, mathematics, and engineering principles to solve problems related to the production and use of chemicals, fuels, drugs, food, and other products.

courses.name: Economics
courses.faculty: Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences
courses.description: NUS Economics provides rigorous training in economic theory, quantitative methods, and policy analysis. Students develop strong analytical skills applicable to finance, consulting, government, and research.

courses.name: Political Science
courses.faculty: Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences
courses.description: NUS Political Science examines political institutions, governance, international relations, and public policy. Students develop critical thinking and analytical skills for careers in government, diplomacy, and advocacy.

courses.name: English Language and Literature
courses.faculty: Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences
courses.description: NUS English explores literature, linguistics, and cultural studies. Students develop advanced skills in critical analysis, communication, and creative expression through the study of diverse literary traditions.

courses.name: Psychology
courses.faculty: Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences
courses.description: NUS Psychology covers cognitive, developmental, social, and clinical psychology. Students learn research methods and statistical analysis alongside psychological theory, preparing them for careers in counselling, HR, and research.

courses.name: Data Science and Analytics
courses.description: NUS Data Science and Analytics combines statistics, computer science, and domain expertise. Students learn data wrangling, machine learning, statistical modelling, and data visualisation for extracting insights from complex datasets.

courses.name: Information Systems
courses.description: NUS Information Systems bridges business and technology, training students to design and manage IT solutions for organisations. The curriculum covers systems analysis, project management, enterprise architecture, and digital innovation.

courses.name: Accountancy
courses.description: NUS Accountancy provides a professional accounting education accredited by ISCA, CPA Australia, and ACCA. Students learn financial reporting, auditing, taxation, and management accounting.

courses.name: Life Sciences
courses.description: NUS Life Sciences covers molecular biology, genetics, ecology, and biomedical science. Students gain laboratory skills and scientific reasoning applicable to research, healthcare, and the biotech industry.

courses.name: Pharmacy
courses.description: NUS Pharmacy is a four-year programme that trains students in pharmaceutical sciences, pharmacology, and clinical pharmacy practice. Graduates can register as pharmacists in Singapore.

prerequisites.requirementText: H2 Chemistry and H2 Mathematics or H2 Biology or H2 Physics

courses.name: Architecture
courses.description: NUS Architecture is a five-year programme combining design studio work with architectural theory, technology, and history. Students develop skills in spatial design, sustainability, and urban planning.

courses.name: Industrial Design
courses.description: NUS Industrial Design trains students in product design, user experience, and design thinking. The programme combines creative studio practice with engineering and technology for human-centred design solutions.

courses.name: Computer Science
courses.faculty: School of Computer Science and Engineering
courses.description: NTU Computer Science offers a comprehensive curriculum in algorithms, software engineering, AI, cybersecurity, and computer networks. The programme is well-regarded for its strong industry connections and research output.

courses.name: Computer Engineering
courses.faculty: School of Computer Science and Engineering / School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
courses.description: NTU Computer Engineering integrates hardware and software expertise, covering embedded systems, computer architecture, IoT, and real-time computing. Graduates are well-positioned for roles bridging hardware and software.

courses.name: Business
courses.description: NTU Business offers a comprehensive business education with specialisations in banking and finance, marketing, human resource consulting, and entrepreneurship. The programme is triple-accredited (AACSB, EQUIS, AMBA).

courses.name: Accountancy
courses.description: NTU Accountancy provides professional training in financial accounting, auditing, taxation, and corporate governance. The programme is accredited by ISCA, CPA Australia, and ACCA for direct professional qualification pathways.

courses.name: Biological Sciences
courses.description: NTU Biological Sciences covers molecular biology, genetics, cell biology, and ecology. The programme offers specialisations in biomedical sciences and environmental biology with strong laboratory training.

courses.name: Chemistry and Biological Chemistry
courses.faculty: School of Physical and Mathematical Sciences
courses.description: NTU Chemistry and Biological Chemistry provides rigorous training in organic, inorganic, physical, and analytical chemistry. Students gain strong laboratory skills for careers in chemical and pharmaceutical industries.

courses.name: Mechanical Engineering
courses.faculty: School of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
courses.description: NTU Mechanical Engineering covers mechanics, thermodynamics, manufacturing, and robotics. The programme emphasises hands-on design projects and offers specialisations in areas such as robotics and energy systems.

courses.name: Electrical and Electronic Engineering
courses.faculty: School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
courses.description: NTU Electrical and Electronic Engineering covers power systems, microelectronics, signal processing, and communications. Students can specialise in areas including power engineering, intelligent systems, and nanoelectronics.

courses.name: Civil Engineering
courses.faculty: School of Civil and Environmental Engineering
courses.description: NTU Civil Engineering covers structural engineering, geotechnics, water resources, and transportation. The programme prepares students for careers in infrastructure development and environmental engineering.

courses.name: Aerospace Engineering
courses.faculty: School of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
courses.description: NTU Aerospace Engineering covers aerodynamics, propulsion, flight mechanics, and avionics. Students gain expertise in aircraft and spacecraft design, maintenance, and systems integration relevant to Singapore's aviation industry.

courses.name: Materials Engineering
courses.faculty: School of Materials Science and Engineering
courses.description: NTU Materials Engineering covers metals, polymers, ceramics, and nanomaterials. Students learn to develop and characterise advanced materials for applications in electronics, biomedicine, and energy.

prerequisites.requirementText: H2 Mathematics and H2 Chemistry or H2 Physics

courses.name: Communication Studies
courses.faculty: Wee Kim Wee School of Communication and Information
courses.description: NTU Communication Studies covers journalism, advertising, public relations, broadcasting, and cinema. Students develop strong media production and strategic communication skills for the creative industries.

courses.name: Linguistics and Multilingual Studies
courses.description: NTU Linguistics and Multilingual Studies explores language structure, cognitive linguistics, translation, and multilingualism. Students develop expertise in language analysis and multilingual communication relevant to Singapore's diverse context.

courses.name: Art, Design and Media
courses.faculty: School of Art, Design and Media
courses.description: NTU Art, Design and Media offers specialisations in photography and digital imaging, animation, product design, interactive media, and visual communication. The programme balances creative practice with design theory.

courses.name: Economics
courses.description: NTU Economics trains students in microeconomics, macroeconomics, econometrics, and applied economics. The programme emphasises quantitative methods and data analysis for economic policy and business applications.

courses.name: Psychology
courses.description: NTU Psychology covers cognitive, developmental, social, and clinical psychology. The programme emphasises research methods and evidence-based practice for understanding human behaviour and mental processes.

courses.name: Business Management
courses.faculty: Lee Kong Chian School of Business
courses.description: SMU Business Management offers a broad-based business education with majors in finance, marketing, operations management, organisational behaviour, and strategic management. Known for its seminar-style interactive pedagogy.

courses.name: Accountancy
courses.description: SMU Accountancy is one of the top accounting programmes in Asia, offering professional training in financial accounting, audit, tax, and data analytics. The programme provides direct pathways to CPA and ACCA qualifications.

courses.name: Economics
courses.description: SMU Economics focuses on applied economics with strong quantitative training. Students learn econometrics, behavioural economics, and policy analysis through SMU's distinctive seminar-style teaching approach.

courses.name: Information Systems
courses.faculty: School of Computing and Information Systems
courses.description: SMU Information Systems trains students in software development, cybersecurity, data management, and digital business transformation. The programme is known for producing graduates with strong technical and business acumen.

courses.name: Law
courses.faculty: Yong Pung How School of Law
courses.description: SMU Law offers a four-year LLB programme with a distinctive focus on commercial and corporate law, dispute resolution, and cross-border transactions. The seminar-style teaching develops strong advocacy and communication skills.

courses.name: Social Sciences
courses.description: SMU Social Sciences offers majors in politics and public policy, psychology, and sociology. The programme develops critical thinking and research skills through SMU's interactive seminar-based pedagogy.

courses.name: Computing and Law
courses.faculty: School of Computing and Information Systems / Yong Pung How School of Law
courses.description: SMU Computing and Law is a unique double-degree programme combining computer science with legal studies. Graduates are uniquely positioned for roles at the intersection of technology and law, including tech regulation, IP law, and legal tech.
